text:
There once was a wise old tortoise that lived near a babbling brook. Every morning, the tortoise would saunter into the forest to bask in the sunlight and ponder life's mysteries. As the seasons changed, the woodland creatures varied in size. There was the tiny ground squirrel who loved nuts, the towering pine owl who rarely left his nest, and the mossy turtle shell dragon that liked to sleep on the rocks.

One day, after a particularly rainy afternoon when all the ground squirrels were indoors poring over ancient scrolls, an idea popped into the noggin' of Olaf the Owl Owl. He wanted to host a gathering of all the animals in the forest, to celebrate their friendship. Olaf decided that the best place would be under the large oak tree where the biggest oak-apple flower had bloomed the spring before.

That evening, a host of woodland creatures had gathered. There were fluffy cottonwoods bobbing their heads as they listened to Olaf's tales
